notes.anchor: when you have given your attention to Sextus' auction
notes.note: Sextus Peducaeus, son of the propraetor of Sicily and a high-principled friend of both Cicero and Atticus, here had property going under the hammer that Atticus was helping manage. Cicero waits on that business before expecting Atticus at Astura.

notes.anchor: if I had taken sufficient counsel on a certain matter
notes.note: Cicero's recurrent reluctance, in his grief, to return to Rome; the unspecified matter is the financing and siting of the planned shrine for Tullia. He prefers Atticus to come to him at Astura.
